closureNote: The terminal engine program establishes obligation governance, incremental analysis, closed-world bundling, differential corpora, generated workflow contracts, unified typed error mapping, snapshot-bound execution, SDK and FFI parity across NAPI/WASM/CLI/LSP, and explicit public/debug response partitions. The entries below preserve the former residual identities together with their completion evidence.

entries.scope: Open-workspace, snapshot, query, diagnostics, build, and explain execute over one snapshot-bound workspace runtime.
The LSP request adapter drives all five workflows and binds published diagnostics to the same workspace snapshot.
Representative input, workspace, resolution, and unsupported failures have structural parity across all four adapters.
NAPI, WASM, and CLI normalize an empty style path through the shared workspace runtime; LSP is explicitly not applicable because document identity requires a URI.
Optional typed error evidence references are accepted only when their node keys exist in the durable evidence graph and remain absent from default payloads.
Build source-map mappings and explain original-source identities are compared structurally across all four adapters.
The shared differential oracle executes five workflows and four real adapters with committed coverage and representative error matrices.
